name: Data Collection
description: Learn about Data Collection and its applications in physics.

content.overview: Data collection is carefully gathering your measurements. Not just 'oh I measured something' but recording it properly so you can use it.
content.explanation:
Good data collection means:

**1. Be careful and precise.** Use your tools correctly. Read measuring devices at eye level. Don't rush.

**2. Record immediately.** Write down measurements right away. Don't trust memory. Write the actual number, not 'about 5'—write 5.2.

**3. Be consistent.** Measure the same way every time. If measuring height, always measure from the same point. If timing, always use the same method.

**4. Record units.** '5' alone is useless. '5 meters' means something. Always include units.

**5. Do multiple trials.** One measurement is luck. Three or more is data.

**6. Watch for mistakes.** If one measurement looks way different, note it. Maybe something went wrong—mark it!

**Example:** Measuring pendulum period
- Wrong: 'Period is about 2 seconds'
- Right: Trial 1: 2.04 s, Trial 2: 2.06 s, Trial 3: 2.03 s

Good data collection = data you can trust later.
keyPoints: Record numbers immediately, not from memory
Measure the same way every time
Watch for mistakes or unusual results
Write clearly so others can read it
content.whyItMatters: Bad data collection ruins an experiment. Careful collection saves you time and gives you trustworthy results.
